name: Nsc (Naca Counseling Subsidiary) - Jacksonville, FL
description_short: NACA is a non-profit housing advocacy organization offering character-based mortgage lending and HUD-approved housing counseling to help underserved communities achieve affordable homeownership.
description_long:
NACA (Neighborhood Assistance Corporation of America) was founded in 1988 by the Boston Hotel Workers Union and has spent 36 years working to challenge predatory lending practices and close the racial wealth disparity gap. The organization has served 3 million people and helped 500,000 homeowners achieve affordable homeownership through character-based lending rather than credit score-based underwriting.

NACA offers a distinctive mortgage product called the "Best in America Mortgage" with no down payment, no closing costs or fees, no mortgage insurance, and no credit score consideration. As of April 2026, rates include 5.625% for 30-year fixed, 5.125% for 20-year, and 5% for 15-year mortgages. The organization also provides HUD-approved housing counseling (representing 30% of all HUD housing counseling nationally) and operates "Achieve the Dream" multi-day events in various cities where prospective members can access services and become NACA-qualified.

What distinguishes NACA is its mission-driven approach to lending based on character and circumstances rather than credit scores, combined with robust community advocacy against predatory landlords and corporate real estate investors. The organization maintains $20 billion in mortgage commitments and actively engages members as "NACtivist" volunteers. Their approach fundamentally challenges traditional credit-based lending models by considering borrower circumstances holistically.
 
NACA operates as a legitimate non-profit with a 36-year track record and significant scale (75,000 NACA mortgages issued). However, prospective borrowers should note that qualification requires attending multi-day "Achieve the Dream" events and completing a membership process, which represents a meaningful time commitment compared to traditional mortgage lenders. The organization is headquartered nationally with regional operations, and while Jacksonville, FL is mentioned, the website emphasizes their national reach through scheduled events.
pros: Zero down payment requirement on mortgages — eliminates a major barrier to homeownership
No closing costs or fees — transparent pricing with no hidden charges
No mortgage insurance required — saves thousands over loan life
Character-based lending ignores credit scores — serves borrowers with poor credit histories
Below-market fixed rates — competitive pricing (5.625% 30-year as of April 2026)
HUD-approved housing counseling — provides education and guidance before/after purchase
36-year track record with 500,000 homeowners helped — demonstrates organizational stability and success
cons: Requires attendance at multi-day 'Achieve the Dream' events — significant time commitment (Friday-Sunday) to qualify
Limited location accessibility — events held only in select cities (Hartford, Little Rock, Milwaukee, Fort Myers, St. Louis)
Membership process appears lengthy — website emphasizes file access and document uploads with status checking, suggesting extended qualification timeline
No clear information on loan amounts or property type restrictions — limited transparency on underwriting parameters beyond credit score waiver
Mission-driven model may limit loan volume availability — 75,000 mortgages for 500,000 homeowners suggests not all applicants are served
best_for: Low-to-moderate income borrowers with poor or no credit history seeking first-time homeownership
Borrowers priced out of traditional mortgages due to down payment requirements or closing costs
Community members within driving distance of scheduled Achieve the Dream events
Homebuyers aligned with NACA's mission of economic equity and community advocacy
diagnosis: NACA is best for low-to-moderate income first-time homebuyers with poor credit who can commit to attending regional Achieve the Dream events and align with the organization's community advocacy mission. The primary caveat is that qualifying requires substantial upfront time investment (multi-day event attendance) and geographic feasibility, making this inaccessible for borrowers outside event service areas or unable to take time off work.
services: Character-based mortgage lending without credit score consideration
No closing costs or mortgage insurance mortgages
Multi-day 'Achieve the Dream' qualifying events in multiple cities
Member portal for document uploads and application status tracking
NACtivist volunteer opportunities and community engagement
Vendor partnerships (lenders, servicers, real estate agents, home inspectors)
Predatory lending advocacy and tenant support services
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 3986 Boulevard Center Dr Ste 101, Jacksonville, FL 32207
meta_description: NACA offers character-based mortgage lending and HUD housing counseling for underserved communities.
